TRIM37–PARP1–TET1 axis maintains stemness and prevents osteoporosis by inhibiting DNMT1 alternative splicing via 5hmC regulation

evidence_entities.text: The significance of DNA hydroxymethylation in replicative senescence of mesenchymal stem cells (MSCs) and aging-related osteoporosis remains unknown.
PARP1 recruits TET1 to hydrolyze methylated nucleotides on DNMT1 exons, aiding CTCF in preventing DNMT1 alternative splicing in early MSCs.
ATM phosphorylates TRIM37 at Th203, promoting its nuclear entry and the monoubiquitination of PARP1, stabilizing the protein.
Early human MSCs show higher levels of 5hmC, thereby exhibiting increased proliferation and differentiation potential
Co-treatment with resveratrol (ATM activator) and vitamin C (TET1 activator) rejuvenates late MSCs via the TRIM37/PARP1/DNMT1 pathway and alleviates osteoporosis in aged mice.
